engineCodes.h2: Subaru Engine Codes & Average Rebuilt Prices (UK Supply)
engineCodes.h3: Compare costs for every major Subaru engine — EJ Series Boxer, FA/FB modern petrol, and EE20 Boxer diesel. All prices are typical UK market ranges for professionally rebuilt engines with warranty. Click "Get Quote" on any engine to receive tailored prices from our network of UK Subaru specialists.

groups.name: EJ Series – Classic Boxer Petrol
groups.failureNote: [Get Quote →] *(per row – developer to implement as button pre-filling engine code)* Common failure points (expandable note): EJ Series engines are known for head gasket failure (1999–2011 NA 2.5L) and piston ringland/rod bearing failure on turbo variants. Our rebuilt units include upgraded multi-layer steel gaskets, forged pistons, and balanced rotating assemblies to address these weaknesses.

groups.name: FA/FB Series – Modern Direct-Injection Boxer
groups.failureNote: [Get Quote →] *(per row)* Common failure points (expandable note): FA/FB engines are known for timing chain tensioner wear (early FA20) and oil consumption (FB20). Our rebuilt units include updated tensioners, revised piston rings, and full gasket sets to ensure long-term reliability.

groups.name: EE20 – Boxer Diesel
groups.failureNote: [Get Quote →] Common failure points (expandable note): EE20 Boxer diesel engines are known for catastrophic crankshaft failure due to insufficient oiling at high mileage. Our rebuilt units include upgraded crankshafts, reinforced connecting rods, and revised oil pump assemblies to prevent recurrence.

groups.name: EZ Series – H6 Flat-Six
groups.failureNote: [Get Quote →] *(per row)* Common failure points (expandable note): EZ H6 engines are known for oil consumption and timing chain guide wear at high mileage. Our rebuilt units include updated guide materials, revised valve stem seals, and full gasket refreshes to restore factory performance.

groups.name: Legacy & Specialist Codes
groups.failureNote: [Get Quote →] *(per row)* Common failure points (expandable note): Legacy EJ codes are prone to head gasket and oil seal wear. Our rebuilt units include upgraded sealing components and balanced internals for classic Subaru reliability. Closing note: Not sure which engine code your Subaru uses? Enter your registration number and we'll match you to the exact engine and best quote — instantly. All engines supplied with a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Subaru specialists. Developer Notes: - Each "Get Quote →" button should pre-fill the engine code into the quote request form and trigger a modal or redirect to the quote flow. - Implement expandable failure notes using `<details>`/`<summary>` or JS accordion for mobile-friendly UX. - Add `data-engine-code` attributes to each row for analytics tracking and future deep-linking to dedicated engine pages (e.g., `/subaru/ej25-engine-replacement`). - Lazy-load table groups below the fold to preserve Core Web Vitals. - Ensure price ranges are rendered from a dynamic config file for easy updates without code changes.

commonProblems.h2: Common Subaru Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Subaru's iconic Boxer layout delivers brilliant handling, but certain engine families carry well-documented failure patterns that drive replacement demand. The naturally-aspirated EJ25 (1999–2011), the EE20 boxer diesel (2008–2016), and turbocharged EJ257/FA20 units account for the majority of urgent searches on EngineMarket. Every rebuilt engine supplied through our network includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point — plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the most common Subaru engine problems with realistic UK repair costs and honest guidance on when replacement is the smarter choice.

problems.h4: EJ25 Head Gasket Failure – Why Naturally-Aspirated 2.5L Boxers Leak Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Forester, Outback, Legacy, Impreza (1999–2011, EJ251, EJ252, EJ253, EJ254)
problems.rootCause: The original multi-layer steel (MLS) head gasket design on early EJ25 engines was prone to external coolant leaks due to uneven cylinder head clamping pressure and thermal cycling. Over time, coolant seeps into the combustion chamber or oil galleries, leading to overheating or oil contamination.
problems.recommendation: If your EJ25 is showing early coolant seepage but hasn't overheated, a specialist preventative gasket refresh offers the best value. However, once overheating has occurred or oil is contaminated, the risk of hidden damage makes a full rebuilt engine the more reliable long-term investment — especially on Foresters and Outbacks still worth £3,000–£7,000.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt EJ25 engine prices for your Forester or Outback

problems.h4: Boxer Diesel Crankshaft Failure – The EE20 Engine's Critical Weakness
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Forester, Outback, Legacy, XV (2008–2016, EE20 2.0L boxer diesel)
problems.rootCause: The EE20's crankshaft design, combined with oiling limitations under sustained load, can lead to premature bearing wear and catastrophic crankshaft fracture. Once knock is audible, the damage is usually terminal — the crank cannot be reliably repaired.
problems.recommendation: There is no economical "repair" tier for EE20 crankshaft failure — once knock is audible, the engine is terminal. If your diesel Forester or Outback is otherwise in good condition, a rebuilt EE20 from our specialist network offers the most cost-effective path back to the road, with upgraded internals to prevent recurrence.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for rebuilt EE20 boxer diesel engines with uprated crankshafts

problems.group: EJ257 Piston Ringland & Rod Bearing Failure
problems.h4: EJ257 Piston Ringland & Rod Bearing Failure – Turbo STI Engines Under Stress
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Impreza WRX STI, Forester XT, Legacy GT (2004–2014, EJ255, EJ257 turbo)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 40,000–90,000 miles (heavily dependent on tuning and maintenance)
problems.rootCause: High cylinder pressures from aggressive tuning, combined with factory piston ringland design and occasional oil starvation under hard cornering, can cause ringland fracture or spun rod bearings. Knock is the first audible symptom — and by then, damage is often extensive.
problems.recommendation: For STI owners planning to keep the car for performance use, investing in a forged rebuild or reconditioned engine with upgraded internals is the only way to ensure long-term reliability. If the car is a daily driver with moderate use, a quality reconditioned EJ257 from our network offers excellent value with warranty protection.
problems.cta: → Compare forged EJ257 rebuild prices for your Impreza STI

problems.group: FA20 Timing Chain & Valve Spring Issues
problems.h4: FA20 Timing Chain & Valve Spring Issues – Early 2.0L Petrol Boxers
problems.affectedModels: Subaru BRZ, Toyota GT86, Impreza, Forester, XV (2012–2014, FA20, FB20)
problems.rootCause: A production error in early FA20/FB20 valve springs could cause fracture under load, risking valve drop and catastrophic engine damage. Separately, timing chain tensioner wear can cause chain stretch, leading to poor timing and potential interference damage.
problems.recommendation: First, check if your FA20/FB20 is covered by Subaru's valve spring recall — if so, the repair is free. If your engine has audible chain rattle or has exceeded 90,000 miles without a timing service, invest in the intermediate chain replacement. Only opt for full replacement if damage has already occurred.
problems.cta: → Check recall eligibility and compare FA20 engine replacement quotes

problems.group: FB-Series Oil Consumption & Piston Ring Wear
problems.h4: FB-Series Oil Consumption & Piston Ring Wear – The "Burns Oil" Boxer
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Forester, Outback, XV, Impreza (2012–present, FB20, FB25)
problems.rootCause: Early FB-series engines can suffer from piston ring design that allows excess oil to enter the combustion chamber, especially under sustained high-RPM driving. While Subaru considers up to 1 quart per 1,200 miles "normal", many owners experience higher consumption leading to fouled plugs and catalytic damage.
problems.recommendation: Start with the minor PCV and oil service if consumption is mild (<1 quart/1,500 miles). If consumption exceeds this or you notice blue exhaust smoke, the intermediate ring replacement offers good value. For high-mileage vehicles or when catalytic damage is present, a rebuilt FB engine is the most reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt FB20/FB25 engine prices for your Forester or Outback

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru
finalCta.paragraph: Every problem group above shares a common truth: Subaru engines are repairable, but the right solution depends on catching the issue early and choosing a specialist who understands Boxer engineering. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Subaru specialists — all offering supply & fit, upgraded components that address the original failure point, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. With nationwide delivery and transparent quotes, you can get back on the road without paying the main dealer premium. → All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru model and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty — always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier. engineTypes.h2: Subaru Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Subaru engine type available through our UK network, with typical price ranges and recommendations based on your vehicle's age, condition and your budget.

types.title: Subaru Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM‑spec parts. Best for owners who want factory reliability without the factory price — ideal for addressing EJ25 head gasket failures with upgraded MLS gaskets included.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £1,500 – £4,500 (depending on engine code)

types.title: Subaru Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points – such as reinforced piston ringlands for turbo EJ257/FA20 engines and uprated crankshaft assemblies for EE20 Boxer diesel units – for a longer service life than a standard replacement.

types.title: Used Subaru Engines
types.description: A tested, low‑mileage take‑out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority, with verified history and warranty available — suitable for older Forester or Outback models where a full rebuild isn't economical.

types.title: Subaru Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory‑spec, as‑new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for performance models like WRX STI or owners wanting a zero‑mile Boxer engine with enhanced durability.

types.title: Subaru Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn't had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for older or budget‑sensitive models like Impreza 1.6/2.0 NA where a full recon isn't economical.

types.title: Subaru Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Subaru specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, and a workmanship warranty — essential for Boxer engines requiring precise alignment and torque procedures.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £500 – £1,500 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Subaru engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12‑month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Subaru model today.

engineSizes.h2: Subaru Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model
engineSizes.intro: Every Subaru engine size explained — with the models and engine codes that used each displacement — so you can quickly identify the correct replacement unit for your vehicle.

items.title: Subaru 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Subaru's only diesel offering for the UK market, the 2.0L EE20 boxer diesel powered Forester, Outback, Legacy and XV models from 2008–2016. While offering strong torque and AWD capability, this engine family is defined by a critical weakness: crankshaft failure under sustained load, which is terminal and drives the majority of replacement demand for Subaru diesel owners. Engine code(s): EE20 Compatible models (UK): Forester, Outback, Legacy, XV (2008–2016) Production years: 2008–2016 Common failure points: Crankshaft fracture due to oiling limitations under load; terminal failure requiring full replacement
items.cta: Get quotes for Subaru 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Subaru 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.5L EJ15 boxer powered early Impreza and Justy models in the UK. While less common in the replacement market due to lower performance demands, these units can suffer from head gasket seepage on high-mileage examples and remain a budget-conscious option for owners of older non-turbo Subarus. Engine code(s): EJ15 Compatible models (UK): Impreza 1.5, Justy (limited UK availability) Production years: 1992–2007 Common failure points: Head gasket external leaks on high-mileage units; generally robust with routine maintenance
items.cta: Compare Subaru 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L displacement spans both the older EJ16 and newer FB16 boxer families, fitting Impreza, XV and entry-level Forester models. FB16 engines are more prone to oil consumption from piston ring design, while EJ16 units are generally reliable provided timing belt services are maintained — both remain popular for cost-effective replacements. Engine code(s): EJ16, FB16 Compatible models (UK): Impreza, XV, Forester (1992–present) Production years: 1992–present Common failure points: FB16 – piston ring wear leading to oil consumption; EJ16 – timing belt neglect, generally reliable otherwise
items.cta: Compare Subaru 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L EJ18 boxer served Legacy and Impreza models through the 1990s and early 2000s. Though largely superseded by larger displacements, these engines remain in circulation and can develop external head gasket seepage; timely timing belt replacement is critical to avoid catastrophic interference damage. Engine code(s): EJ18 Compatible models (UK): Legacy, Impreza (1990s–early 2000s) Production years: 1990–2005 Common failure points: Head gasket external leaks on later EJ18 units; timing belt service critical to prevent valve damage
items.cta: Compare Subaru 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The most versatile Subaru displacement, the 2.0L boxer spans naturally-aspirated (FB20, FA20), turbo performance (EJ205, EJ207, FA20F) and hybrid applications. EJ20 turbo units are prone to ringland failure under aggressive tuning, while FA20 engines carry a valve spring recall and timing chain tensioner wear — making this size the most frequently searched for replacement across performance and daily-driver segments. Engine code(s): EJ20, EJ205, EJ207, FB20, FA20, FA20F Compatible models (UK): Impreza WRX/STI, Forester XT, Legacy GT, BRZ, XV (1992–present) Production years: 1992–present Common failure points: EJ20 turbo – piston ringland failure under load; FA20 – valve spring recall (early production), timing chain tensioner wear
items.cta: Compare Subaru 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru 2.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.2L EJ22 boxer powered first-generation Legacy and Outback models in the UK. Known for robust construction and simpler engineering than later EJ25 units, these engines rarely suffer catastrophic failure but can develop head gasket seepage on high-mileage examples — making them a reliable, cost-effective replacement option for classic Subaru owners. Engine code(s): EJ22 Compatible models (UK): Legacy, Outback (1990s–early 2000s) Production years: 1990–2001 Common failure points: Generally robust; occasional head gasket seepage on high-mileage units; timing belt replacement essential
items.cta: Compare Subaru 2.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru 2.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The newer 2.4L FA24 turbo boxer powers the Outback XT and select global models, with limited UK availability from 2020 onward. Early data suggests a robust design with improved thermal management over the EJ257, though long-term reliability in UK conditions is still being established — replacement demand remains low but specialist supply is available. Engine code(s): FA24 Compatible models (UK): Outback XT (limited UK availability) Production years: 2020–present Common failure points: Early data suggests robust design; long-term UK reliability still being established
items.cta: Compare Subaru 2.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The dominant Subaru displacement in the UK replacement market, the 2.5L boxer spans naturally-aspirated (EJ251/2/3, FB25) and turbo performance (EJ255, EJ257) applications. EJ25 NA engines (1999–2011) are defined by head gasket failure, while turbo EJ257 units suffer ringland and rod bearing failure under load — making this size the single highest-volume search term for Subaru engine replacement. Engine code(s): EJ25, EJ251, EJ252, EJ253, EJ255, EJ257, FB25 Compatible models (UK): Forester, Outback, Legacy, Impreza WRX/STI (1999–present) Production years: 1999–present Common failure points: EJ25 NA – head gasket failure (1999–2011); EJ257 turbo – piston ringland and rod bearing failure; FB25 – oil consumption from piston ring design
items.cta: Compare Subaru 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0L EZ30 flat-six powered premium Outback, Legacy and Tribeca models from 2000–2009. While offering smooth power and refined operation, these H6 units can develop head gasket seepage on high-mileage examples and require specialist knowledge for replacement — but remain a sought-after option for owners prioritising refinement over fuel economy. Engine code(s): EZ30 (H6) Compatible models (UK): Outback, Legacy, Tribeca (2000–2009) Production years: 2000–2009 Common failure points: Generally reliable; head gasket seepage on high-mileage H6 units; timing belt service critical
items.cta: Compare Subaru 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.6L EZ36 flat-six succeeded the EZ30 in Outback, Legacy and Tribeca models from 2008–2019. Featuring a timing chain (not belt) and improved thermal management, the EZ36 is among Subaru's most robust designs — though head gasket seepage can still occur on very high-mileage examples, driving occasional replacement demand. Engine code(s): EZ36 (H6) Compatible models (UK): Outback, Legacy, Tribeca (2008–2019) Production years: 2008–2019 Common failure points: Robust design; occasional head gasket seepage on very high-mileage units; timing chain reduces maintenance burden
items.cta: Compare Subaru 3.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru 2.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: Subaru's e-BOXER mild hybrid system pairs the FB20 2.0L boxer with an electric motor for improved efficiency in Forester and XV models from 2019 onward. Replacement decisions require specialist hybrid diagnostics — battery health inspection is critical, and the electric assist motor is rarely the failure point; most demand relates to the petrol engine component. System / motor code(s): FB20 + e-BOXER electric motor Compatible models (UK): Forester e-BOXER, XV e-BOXER (2019–present) Production years: 2019–present Important notes: Mild hybrid system assists petrol engine; battery health inspection critical for replacement decisions
items.cta: Enquire about Subaru 2.0L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Subaru 2.5L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The larger-displacement e-BOXER variant uses the FB25 2.5L boxer with the same mild hybrid architecture, available on select Forester trims in the UK. As with the 2.0L hybrid, replacement focus is typically on the petrol engine component; the electric motor and battery pack are reliable but require specialist handling for diagnostics or supply. System / motor code(s): FB25 + e-BOXER electric motor Compatible models (UK): Forester e-BOXER (select trims) Production years: 2019–present Important notes: Same e-BOXER architecture as 2.0L; replacement requires specialist hybrid diagnostics
items.cta: Enquire about Subaru 2.5L hybrid engine availability

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Subaru Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Subaru engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Subaru specialists.

entries.title: EJ253 2.5L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The naturally aspirated EJ253 powered UK Forester, Outback, and Legacy models from 2005 to 2011. Catastrophic failure is overwhelmingly driven by premature multi-layer steel head gasket degradation, leading to coolant loss, overheating, and eventual cylinder head warping. Professionally rebuilt EJ253 units include reinforced MLS gaskets, upgraded cylinder head studs, and precision-machined deck surfaces to permanently resolve this factory weakness.
entries.cta: Compare EJ253 Subaru engine prices for Forester & Outback

entries.title: EJ257 2.5L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Fitted to Impreza WRX STI (2004–2018) and select Legacy GT variants, the EJ257 is the highest-performance factory Boxer in the UK. High boost pressure and aggressive timing cause ringland cracking, piston crown failure, and spun rod bearings, particularly on modified or poorly tuned examples. Quality rebuilt EJ257 engines feature forged pistons and rings, ACL performance bearings, and ported oil galleries to withstand elevated cylinder pressures.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EJ257 Subaru STI engine replacement

entries.title: EJ205 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The EJ205 turbocharged variant equipped early UK WRX and Forester XT models (2001–2005) with reliable mid-range torque. Turbo heat soak, detonation from incorrect fuel mapping, and inadequate cooling frequently result in piston ringland damage and exhaust valve burning. Rebuilt EJ205 long blocks are supplied with balanced rotating assemblies, upgraded intercooler piping provisions, and high-temperature valve seats to restore factory reliability.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt EJ205 Subaru WRX engine deals

entries.title: EJ207 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The high-spec JDM EJ207 was imported into UK STI and Legacy GT models, prized for its forged internals and quick-spooling turbo response. Oil starvation under hard cornering and inadequate piston-to-wall clearances lead to rod bearing failure and piston scuffing at high RPM. Specialist rebuilds incorporate nitrided crankshafts, oversized main bearings, and revised baffle plates to maintain oil pressure during aggressive driving.
entries.cta: Compare EJ207 Subaru turbo engine replacement costs

entries.title: EJ255 2.5L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Powering the Forester XT, Legacy GT, and Outback XT (2004–2013), the EJ255 turbo Boxer delivered accessible performance across Subaru’s crossover range. Weak ring seals and turbo oil feed line leaks cause excessive oil consumption, carbon buildup, and eventual turbocharger seizure. Reconditioned EJ255 engines include revised oil feed lines, low-tension piston rings, and upgraded turbo thrust bearings to extend service intervals.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EJ255 Subaru XT engine replacement

entries.title: EJ251/252 2.5L Petrol
entries.description: Engine These early 2000s naturally aspirated variants (1999–2005) share the EJ25 architecture but feature slightly different head bolt patterns and lower compression ratios. Head gasket failure on the passenger-side cylinder is the dominant replacement driver, compounded by degraded thermostat housings causing thermal cycling. Rebuilt EJ251/252 units are fitted with phase-2 MLS gaskets, reinforced coolant jackets, and updated water pump impellers to prevent repeat failure.

entries.title: EJ22 2.2L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The EJ22 was widely used in 1990s Impreza and Legacy models before the 2.5L became standard, renowned for its robust open-deck block design. Age-related timing belt failure, degraded oil pump gears, and camshaft lobe wear are the primary reasons these engines enter the replacement market today. Specialist-rebuilt EJ22 units include high-capacity oil pumps, heavy-duty timing belt kits, and reprofiled camshafts for reliable daily driving.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt EJ22 Subaru engine prices

entries.title: EJ18 1.8L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Primarily found in early UK Impreza and Legacy saloons (1993–2000), the EJ18 was an economy-focused Boxer with modest power output. Worn valve guides, failing head gaskets, and carburettor-era intake manifold leaks drive replacement demand as these vehicles reach classic mileage thresholds. Reconditioned EJ18 engines feature refreshed cylinder heads, reinforced valve train components, and upgraded intake seals for consistent idle and emissions compliance.
entries.cta: Compare EJ18 Subaru replacement engine costs

entries.title: FA20F 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The direct-injection turbo FA20F replaced the EJ series in WRX, Levorg, and Outback XT from 2014 onward, featuring a more compact combustion chamber. Piston ringland cracking under ECU knock correction and timing chain tensioner wear at higher mileages are the most common failure triggers. Factory-spec rebuilt FA20F units include forged replacement pistons, heavy-duty chain tensioners, and revised high-pressure fuel pump seals.
entries.cta: Get quotes for FA20F Subaru DIT engine replacement

entries.title: FB20 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Used across UK XV, Forester, and Legacy models (2012–2018), the FB20 introduced a longer stroke and improved emissions compliance over the EJ architecture. Excessive oil consumption due to narrow piston ring landings and AVCS (active valve control) phaser rattle frequently prompt owners to seek full replacement. Rebuilt FB20 engines are supplied with upgraded piston ring sets, revised phaser assemblies, and reinforced cylinder wall honing patterns.
entries.cta: Compare FB20 Subaru petrol engine prices

entries.title: FA20D 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The naturally aspirated FA20D powers UK BRZ and Toyota 86 coupes (2012–2020), optimised for high-RPM response and lightweight handling. Dual-mass flywheel failure, timing chain stretch, and valve spring fatigue under track use are the primary replacement drivers for enthusiast owners. Specialist FA20D rebuilds include reinforced valve springs, upgraded chain guides, and precision-balanced crankshaft assemblies for sustained high-rev reliability.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt FA20D Subaru BRZ engine deals

entries.title: FA24 2.4L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Introduced in 2022 for the latest WRX, Outback XT, and Forester Sport, the FA24 increases displacement for improved low-end torque and reduced turbo lag. Early UK reports highlight PCV valve clogging, direct injection carbon buildup, and intercooler condensation ingestion as emerging replacement triggers. Rebuilt FA24 units include upgraded PCV filtration systems, revised intake manifold geometry, and reinforced turbo drain lines.
entries.cta: Check FA24 Subaru turbo engine availability

entries.title: FB25 2.5L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The FB25 equips higher-spec Forester, Outback, and Legacy models (2013–2019), offering improved fuel economy and lower emissions than its EJ25 predecessor. Cam follower wear, oil pump drive chain slack, and intake manifold gasket degradation drive replacement enquiries from high-mileage owners. Reconditioned FB25 engines feature reinforced drive chains, heavy-duty cam followers, and multi-layer intake gaskets for consistent performance.
entries.cta: Get quotes for FB25 Subaru Outback engine replacement

entries.title: FB16 1.6L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Fitted to entry-level UK XV and Impreza models (2013–2018), the FB16 prioritises tax efficiency and urban drivability over outright performance. Timing chain tensioner leakage, degraded spark plugs, and EGR valve carbonisation frequently necessitate full engine swaps rather than piecemeal repairs. Rebuilt FB16 units include upgraded tensioner pistons, refreshed ignition coils, and cleaned EGR passages for reliable daily operation.
entries.cta: Compare FB16 Subaru XV engine replacement costs

entries.title: EE20 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: Engine The world’s first horizontally opposed diesel engine, the EE20 powered Forester, Outback, Legacy, and XV diesel variants (2008–2016) with exceptional low-speed torque and AWD traction. Crankshaft main bearing failure due to inadequate oil pressure at low RPM is the definitive catastrophic weakness, often resulting in terminal block seizure. Specialist rebuilt EE20 engines feature nitrided crankshafts, oversized main bearings, and reinforced oil pump assemblies to permanently resolve the lubrication deficit.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EE20 Subaru Boxer diesel replacement

entries.title: EZ36 3.6L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The EZ36 H6 delivered smooth, vibration-free power to UK Outback, Legacy, and Tribeca models (2007–2019), bridging the gap between four-cylinder efficiency and V8 torque. Oil consumption from hardened valve stem seals, timing chain guide wear, and oil pan baffle degradation are the most common replacement drivers. Rebuilt EZ36 units include Viton valve stem seals, reinforced timing chain guides, and upgraded baffled sumps for consistent oil pickup.
entries.cta: Compare EZ36 Subaru H6 engine replacement prices

entries.title: EZ30 3.0L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The predecessor to the EZ36, the EZ30 equipped 2000–2009 Legacy and Outback 3.0R variants with refined highway cruising capability. Timing belt tensioner collapse, alternator bracket failure, and degraded water pump impellers frequently lead to catastrophic overheating if not addressed promptly. Reconditioned EZ30 engines feature heavy-duty tensioners, reinforced accessory brackets, and upgraded coolant circulation pumps for reliable long-distance touring. Closing line (Block A): Can’t find your engine code listed above? Use our full Subaru engine code directory below — every code from the last 25 years — or enter your registration number for an instant match to the correct replacement engine with warranty.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt EZ30 Subaru 3.0 engine deals

engineCodeDirectory.closing: Can’t find your engine code listed above? Use our full Subaru engine code directory below — every code from the last 25 years — or enter your registration number for an instant match to the correct replacement engine with warranty.

directory.h3: Full Subaru Engine Code Directory (All Years)
directory.label: “View all Subaru engine codes (40+ codes, 1990–2025)”
directory.intro: A comprehensive alphabetical listing of every Subaru engine code from the last 25 years. Use the search above or enter your registration for an instant match to your specific engine. All codes link to a quote request.

engineYears.h2: Subaru Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When

years.preview: 2002 — EJ-Series Boxer Launch: EJ20, EJ22 & Phase 1 EJ25 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: The EJ-series flat-four Boxer engine launched in the Subaru Legacy in 1989 and rapidly became the defining technical identity of the brand — powering every model from the Impreza and Forester to the SVX coupé, and establishing Subaru's signature low centre of gravity and Symmetrical AWD philosophy. Key Changes EJ20 2.0-litre DOHC/SOHC Boxer introduced from 1989 across Legacy, Impreza and SVX — the first high-output EJ unit and the engine that would carry WRX development for the next three decades EJ22 2.2-litre SOHC introduced from 1990 in Legacy — a more torquey, closed-deck variant regarded as the most structurally robust naturally aspirated EJ of the era EJ25 2.5-litre Phase 1 DOHC (EJ25D) introduced 1996 in Legacy and Forester — the first open-deck EJ25, and the origin of Subaru's most documented head gasket failure pattern EJ205 (220 PS turbo) and EJ20G (280 PS Group N) introduced in Impreza WRX — establishing the WRX name globally and generating a performance engine following that persists today Symmetrical AWD standardised across all EJ-powered Subaru models by 1996 Main Engines EJ20 SOHC / DOHC — 2.0-litre Boxer (Subaru Legacy, Impreza, Outback) EJ20G / EJ20K — 2.0-litre turbocharged (Subaru Impreza WRX, WRX STI JDM) EJ22 / EJ22T — 2.2-litre SOHC naturally aspirated (Subaru Legacy, Impreza) EJ25D (Phase 1) — 2.5-litre DOHC naturally aspirated (Subaru Legacy, Forester, Outback) Popular Models Subaru Impreza WRX (Mk1 / Mk2 GC/GF) Subaru Legacy 2.0 / 2.2 Subaru Forester S/Turbo (SF5) Subaru Outback 2.5 Known For EJ22 is the gold standard naturally aspirated EJ — closed-deck construction and robust metallurgy make it the most head-gasket-secure non-turbo EJ; beloved by long-term UK owners Phase 1 EJ25D head gasket failure is well-documented and affects a meaningful proportion of non-turbo Legacy and Forester examples from 1996–2002 — internal coolant-to-combustion leaks are the worst-case scenario; MLS gasket replacement is the definitive fix EJ20G and EJ20K turbocharged units in early WRX are highly tunable and broadly dependable when serviced correctly — timing belt and oil pickup attention are the primary preventative maintenance tasks EJ-series Boxer architecture gives Subaru its characteristic low centre of gravity and exhaust note; engineering and enthusiast communities are deeply established in the UK [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: EJ20, EJ20G, EJ20K, EJ201, EJ202, EJ204, EJ22, EJ22T, EJ25D
years.cta: Search for 1989–2002 Subaru EJ20 and EJ25 replacement engines — compare UK specialist rebuilt and remanufactured Boxer options

years.preview: 2011 — EJ207 STI, EJ257 WRX & EJ25 Phase 2: Performance Peak & Gasket Revision 🔴 Major Reliability Revision
years.description: Short intro: The EJ207 fitted to the Impreza WRX STI is widely regarded as the finest naturally-aspirated-derived turbocharged Subaru engine ever built — while Phase 2 EJ25 revisions addressed the worst of the earlier head gasket failures, and the EJ257 brought the 2.5-litre Boxer turbo to the UK WRX platform. Key Changes EJ207 2.0-litre twin-scroll turbocharged DOHC introduced in Impreza WRX STI from 2001 UK — 280 PS (later 300+ PS), forged internals, revised oil circuit; the benchmark Subaru performance engine EJ251 / EJ253 Phase 2 EJ25 SOHC revisions introduced from 2000–2003 — external-leak failure mode only (replacing catastrophic internal leak of Phase 1); MLS gasket upgrades recommended but not yet factory-standard EJ255 2.5-litre DOHC turbo introduced in Forester XT and Legacy GT from 2002 — first open-deck 2.5 turbo, better head gasket compliance than naturally aspirated Phase 1 EJ257 2.5-litre DOHC turbo (305 bhp) introduced in Impreza WRX STI Mk2 (GD/GG) from 2004 UK — replaces EJ207 in the standard WRX STI lineup; EJ207 continues in JDM spec EZ30 3.0-litre horizontally-opposed six-cylinder introduced in Legacy H6 and Outback H6 from 2001 — the smoothest and most refined Subaru engine of the era Main Engines EJ207 — 2.0-litre twin-scroll turbocharged DOHC (Subaru Impreza WRX STI Mk1/Mk2, JDM spec continues) EJ255 — 2.5-litre DOHC turbocharged (Subaru Forester XT, Legacy GT) EJ257 — 2.5-litre DOHC turbocharged 305 bhp (Subaru Impreza WRX STI Mk2/Mk3 from 2004) EJ251 / EJ253 — 2.5-litre SOHC naturally aspirated Phase 2 (Subaru Impreza, Forester, Legacy/Outback) EZ30 — 3.0-litre naturally aspirated flat-six (Subaru Legacy H6, Outback H6) Popular Models Subaru Impreza WRX STI (Mk2 / Mk3 GD/GG/GR/GV) Subaru Impreza WRX (2.0 and 2.5 non-STI) Subaru Forester XT / STI Subaru Legacy GT / 3.0 H6 Subaru Outback 3.0 H6 Known For EJ207 is argued to be the most desirable Subaru engine ever fitted to a road car — precision-engineered forged internals and twin-scroll response deliver exceptional performance; head gasket integrity is good in turbo spec, but rod bearing wear at high RPM is the primary concern on track-used examples EJ257 in the GD/GR-chassis STI carries the same rod bearing sensitivity at sustained high RPM — a class-action lawsuit in the US acknowledged premature bearing failure at tight factory tolerances; aftermarket bearing clearance upgrades are widely adopted by UK specialists Phase 2 EJ25 SOHC resolves the catastrophic internal leak mode but external coolant seepage from 100,000 miles is still common — MLS gasket upgrade remains the standard specialist recommendation EZ30 flat-six is the most underrated Subaru engine in the UK — exceptionally smooth and reliable, with no significant documented failure patterns across its production life [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: EJ207, EJ255, EJ257, EJ251, EJ252, EJ253, EJ254, EZ30
years.cta: Compare 2000–2011 Subaru EJ207 and EJ257 WRX STI replacement engines — UK specialist rebuilt options with uprated bearing clearances available

years.preview: 2013 — EJ20X / EJ20Y Legacy & Final EJ205 Era: Refined EJ Endgame
years.description: Short intro: The mid-2000s Legacy and Outback used the final-generation refined EJ Boxer units — the EJ20X and EJ20Y AVCS-equipped twins — while the EJ205 continued to serve the Impreza WRX non-STI lineup, collectively representing the mature peak of the EJ architecture before the FB family took over. Key Changes EJ20X / EJ20Y 2.0-litre AVCS (Active Valve Control System) dual-AVCS introduced in Legacy GT and Outback from 2003 — most fuel-efficient and refined EJ turbo variant ever offered EJ205 continues in UK-spec Impreza WRX (non-STI) through to 2007 — 220 PS with revised intercooling and AVCS from 2004 EJ253 Phase 2 continues as standard non-turbo across Forester, Impreza and Outback up to 2012 — longest-running EJ25 variant in production Timing belt service interval standardised at 60,000 miles / 5 years across all EJ-series — a critical preventative maintenance milestone for all UK EJ owners EZ36 3.6-litre flat-six introduced from 2007 in Outback and Tribeca — replaces EZ30 as the top-of-range naturally aspirated option Main Engines EJ20X / EJ20Y — 2.0-litre AVCS turbocharged (Subaru Legacy GT, Outback 2.0GT) EJ205 — 2.0-litre turbocharged AVCS (Subaru Impreza WRX non-STI, Forester STI) EJ253 — 2.5-litre SOHC naturally aspirated (Subaru Impreza, Forester X, Legacy/Outback) EZ36 — 3.6-litre naturally aspirated flat-six (Subaru Outback 3.6R, Tribeca 3.6) Popular Models Subaru Legacy GT 2.0 Turbo Subaru Outback 2.0GT / 3.6R Subaru Impreza WRX 2.0 Turbo (non-STI) Subaru Forester 2.5X Subaru Tribeca 3.6 Known For EJ20X / EJ20Y are considered the most refined turbocharged EJ units — dual AVCS gives a broader, more usable power band than earlier variants; head gasket integrity in turbo-spec is notably stronger than naturally aspirated EJ25 EJ253 external coolant seepage remains a documented long-term concern on non-turbo variants — specialist recommendation is MLS gasket upgrade at 100,000 miles as a preventative measure EZ36 flat-six is a durable and refined unit with no significant failure patterns documented across UK use — poorly appreciated but an excellent alternative to the troubled EJ25 Timing belt service neglect is the single most preventable cause of catastrophic EJ engine failure across this entire era — UK specialists report a significant proportion of EJ replacements arrive as belt-failure consequences [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: EJ20X, EJ20Y, EJ205, EJ253, EZ36
years.cta: Get replacement quotes for 2003–2013 Subaru Legacy, Forester and Impreza WRX EJ20 and EJ25 engines — UK specialists available

years.preview: 2021 — FB-Series Launch: Timing Chain, Direct Injection & NA Boxer Evolution 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: The FB-series Boxer family launched from 2011 to replace the EJ-series in naturally aspirated applications — bringing a timing chain, direct injection, and improved thermal efficiency across the Impreza, Forester, XV and Outback, while introducing a new and distinct oil consumption concern that took Subaru several years to fully resolve. Key Changes FB20 2.0-litre DOHC chain-driven Boxer introduced in 2011 Forester and Legacy — critical advancement replacing the timing belt of all EJ units; direct injection added from 2012 FB25 2.5-litre DOHC chain-driven Boxer introduced from 2011 in Legacy, Outback and Forester — naturally aspirated replacement for EJ25 range FB16 1.6-litre DOHC introduced in Subaru Impreza and XV from 2011 — entry-level FB for European and UK markets FB16DIT 1.6-litre turbocharged introduced in Subaru Levorg from 2014 — the first turbocharged FB unit in the UK Oil consumption issue documented on early FB20 and FB25 units (2011–2016) — oil control ring carbon fouling causes consumption up to 1 litre per 1,000 miles; Subaru issued TSB (02-157-14R) acknowledging the problem FB25D revised from 2018 with updated piston rings addressing the oil consumption concern — the definitive resolution Main Engines FB20 — 2.0-litre DOHC chain-driven naturally aspirated Boxer (Subaru Impreza, XV, Crosstrek, Forester, Legacy) FB25 — 2.5-litre DOHC chain-driven naturally aspirated Boxer (Subaru Legacy, Outback, Forester) FB16 — 1.6-litre DOHC naturally aspirated (Subaru Impreza, XV entry) FB16DIT — 1.6-litre DOHC turbocharged (Subaru Levorg GT) FB25D (revised) — 2.5-litre updated Boxer (Subaru Forester, Legacy, Outback from 2018) Popular Models Subaru Forester 2.0 / 2.5 FB Subaru Outback 2.5 FB Subaru Legacy 2.5 FB Subaru Impreza 2.0 FB Subaru XV / Crosstrek 2.0 FB Subaru Levorg 1.6 DIT Known For FB-series timing chain is a landmark improvement over the EJ timing belt — eliminates the most costly catastrophic failure mode of the EJ era; no significant chain-related failure pattern has emerged Early FB20 and FB25 oil consumption is the defining concern for 2011–2016 examples — oil control ring carbon fouling causes progressive consumption; Subaru's own TSB confirms the issue and prescribes a structured diagnosis and ring replacement process FB16DIT in the Levorg is an engaging, torquey unit well-suited to the estate body; timing chain and oil feed quality are the primary maintenance disciplines on high-mileage examples FB25D from 2018 with updated piston rings is regarded by UK specialists as a substantially improved unit — oil consumption concerns are largely absent on post-2018 Forester and Outback examples [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: FB20, FB20D, FB25, FB25D, FB16, FB16DIT, FB20E (e-Boxer)
years.cta: Compare 2011–2021 Subaru FB20 and FB25 Boxer replacement engines — UK specialists with updated ring specifications available

years.preview: 2021 — FA20DIT WRX & FA20 BRZ: Turbo & NA Performance Reinvention 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: The FA20 family introduced an entirely new Subaru performance engine architecture from 2012 — a more modern direct-injection 2.0-litre Boxer replacing EJ performance units in the WRX, while the naturally aspirated FA20 (in partnership with Toyota) powered the BRZ sports car. Key Changes FA20 2.0-litre naturally aspirated D-4S dual-injection introduced in Subaru BRZ (2012) and Toyota GT86 — co-developed with Toyota, rear-wheel drive only, and the first modern Subaru sports car since the SVX FA20DIT 2.0-litre turbocharged direct-injection introduced in Subaru WRX from 2014 (VA body) — replaces EJ205 in non-STI WRX; produces 268 PS standard EJ257 retained in WRX STI VA (2014–2021) alongside the FA20DIT — parallel powertrain strategy across WRX family FA20 BRZ features D-4S combined port and direct injection — resolving the carbon intake fouling concern inherent in pure DI engines Intake duct cracking and stalling recalls issued for FA20DIT 2015–2016 WRX — a documented early production concern addressed by Subaru Main Engines FA20 (D-4S) — 2.0-litre naturally aspirated dual-injection Boxer (Subaru BRZ Mk1, Toyota GT86) FA20DIT — 2.0-litre turbocharged DI Boxer (Subaru WRX VA 2014–2021) EJ257 — 2.5-litre turbocharged (Subaru WRX STI VA, continues in parallel) Popular Models Subaru BRZ Mk1 (ZC6) Subaru WRX FA20DIT (VA) Subaru WRX STI EJ257 (VA) Subaru Forester XT FA20DIT (where fitted) Known For FA20 naturally aspirated in BRZ is a characterful, high-revving unit with no significant documented failure patterns in UK use — the D-4S dual injection largely prevents intake carbon fouling that affects pure DI variants FA20DIT WRX is a torquey and refined turbo unit compared to the EJ it replaced — but ring-land cracking under sustained hard use and oil consumption on higher-mileage examples are concerns that UK specialists now manage routinely EJ257 continues in WRX STI through to 2021 — rod bearing sensitivity at sustained high RPM remains the core specialist advisory; proactive bearing clearance inspection at 60,000 miles is the standard UK specialist recommendation BRZ FA20 community in the UK is well-established with strong aftermarket support — replacement engines are broadly accessible through UK Toyota GT86 parts interchangeability [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: FA20, FA20DIT, FA20F, EJ257 (parallel continuing)
years.cta: Get specialist quotes for Subaru BRZ FA20 and WRX FA20DIT replacement engines — compare UK rebuilt Boxer options now

years.preview: Present — FA24F WRX, Levorg STI & FA24D BRZ: 2.4-Litre Boxer Era 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: The FA24F 2.4-litre turbocharged Boxer introduced from 2017 in the Ascent and Legacy/Outback represents the most significant displacement increase in Subaru's modern performance engine history — culminating in the 2022 WRX's FA24F and the naturally aspirated FA24D in the third-generation BRZ. Key Changes FA24F 2.4-litre turbocharged Boxer introduced in Subaru Ascent (2018) and Legacy/Outback Turbo (2020) — replaces FA20DIT as the flagship turbocharged four-cylinder FA24F adopted in Subaru WRX VB (2022) with 275 PS — the first WRX not offered in STI form, generating significant community debate FA24D 2.4-litre naturally aspirated (D-4S dual injection) introduced in BRZ Mk2 (ZD8) / GR86 from 2022 — 234 PS representing a 34 PS increase over FA20 predecessor CB18 1.8-litre turbocharged Boxer introduced in Levorg GT (2020) and Forester e-Boxer — the most advanced FB-derived unit, producing 174 PS with AVCS and direct injection FA24F in WRX VB uses CVT as sole transmission option in UK spec — replacing the 6-speed manual of the VA WRX; a decision that generated significant enthusiast criticism Main Engines FA24F — 2.4-litre turbocharged Boxer (Subaru WRX VB 2022, Outback XT, Legacy XT, Ascent) FA24D — 2.4-litre naturally aspirated D-4S dual injection (Subaru BRZ Mk2, Toyota GR86) CB18 — 1.8-litre turbocharged DOHC (Subaru Levorg GT, Levorg STI Sport) Popular Models Subaru WRX VB (FA24F) Subaru BRZ Mk2 / Toyota GR86 (FA24D) Subaru Outback 2.4XT Subaru Legacy 2.4XT Subaru Levorg 1.8 GT / STI Sport Known For FA24F in the WRX VB is a torque-rich, smooth-revving unit significantly more refined than the FA20DIT it replaces — early examples accumulating UK mileage show promising reliability; oil consumption and ring-land data is still developing given the relative youth of the fleet FA24D in the BRZ Mk2 is universally praised — the power increase transforms the car's usability without sacrificing the high-revving character that defined its predecessor; dual injection preserves clean intake valves CB18 in the Levorg is a precision-engineered small-displacement turbo Boxer with excellent efficiency; as a relatively new and lower-volume engine in the UK, independent specialist coverage is growing but not yet at the depth of EJ or FA20 knowledge CVT-only WRX VB has polarised the UK Subaru community — but the drivetrain reliability record of CVT-equipped Subaru models is broadly positive across the wider model range [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: FA24F, FA24D, CB18, FA24DIT
years.cta: Source early replacement engines for the Subaru WRX VB FA24F and BRZ Mk2 FA24D — compare UK Boxer specialist quotes with 12-month warranty

years.preview: Present — e-Boxer Hybrid & Solterra EV: Electrification of the Boxer 🔴 Technology Introduction
years.description: Short intro: From 2018, Subaru introduced its e-Boxer self-charging hybrid system — pairing the FB20 or CB18 Boxer engine with a small electric motor integrated into the CVT — before delivering the fully electric Solterra in 2022 on a Toyota-co-developed BEV platform, marking the brand's first step beyond the internal combustion Boxer. Key Changes e-Boxer system introduced in Subaru XV and Forester (2018–2019) — FB20 2.0-litre Boxer acts as primary motive power with a 12.3 kW electric motor providing low-speed EV assistance and energy recovery CB18-based e-Boxer introduced in Levorg GT-H (2020) — more sophisticated integration with the 1.8-litre turbocharged unit Solterra all-electric SUV launched in UK 2022 — co-developed with Toyota bZ4X; 71.4 kWh battery with dual electric motor AWD producing 215 bhp; 289-mile WLTP range All-new Subaru electric models confirmed for UK from 2026 — 380 PS, 74.7 kWh battery, 279-mile range and a Toyota co-developed platform e-Boxer models retain full Symmetrical AWD — 13.6 kW electric motor fitted between gearbox and rear differential maintains Subaru's AWD identity in hybrid form Main Engines FB20 + e-Motor 12.3 kW — e-Boxer hybrid (Subaru XV e-Boxer, Forester e-Boxer) CB18 + e-Motor — e-Boxer hybrid (Subaru Levorg GT-H e-Boxer) Dual PMSM 80 kW front + 80 kW rear — all-electric AWD (Subaru Solterra 71.4 kWh) New 380 PS dual electric motor — (Subaru BEV SUV from 2026) Popular Models Subaru Forester e-Boxer Subaru XV / Crosstrek e-Boxer Subaru Levorg GT-H e-Boxer Subaru Solterra (all variants) Known For e-Boxer system delivers genuine real-world fuel economy improvement in city driving — UK Forester and XV owners report 10–15% improvement versus non-hybrid equivalents e-Boxer's small battery (0.6 kWh usable) means it is a mild assist system rather than a true self-charging hybrid — owners expecting EV-mode running at higher speeds will be disappointed; the system is optimised for low-speed efficiency Solterra is a well-executed entry into the BEV market leveraging Toyota's proven e-TNGA platform — early UK examples report strong reliability; 150 kW rapid charging capability is a genuine usability advantage High-voltage battery servicing for Solterra and e-Boxer requires qualified EV tooling — the Subaru UK dealer network is the primary resource, though Toyota bZ4X parts compatibility assists independent specialist access [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: FB20E (e-Boxer), CB18 e-Boxer, Solterra PMSM 80 kW × 2, 2026 BEV PMSM 380 PS
years.cta: Get ahead of replacement demand for Subaru e-Boxer and Solterra drivetrains — source specialist-backed engines and motors from UK Subaru experts now

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Subaru was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly — all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Subaru specialists.

faq.h2: Subaru Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Subaru owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Subaru engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned Subaru engine typically costs £2,200–£4,800 depending on code (EJ25, FB25, FA20), with performance rebuilds (EJ257, FA20F) ranging £3,800–£7,500. Supply‑only fitting adds £600–£1,500 labour, while main dealers charge 40–60% more. For budget options, used engines start around £800 but carry higher risk. *Always compare three quotes before committing — prices vary significantly by supplier and engine condition.*
items.cta: Compare Subaru engine replacement prices now

items.question: What affects the cost of a Subaru engine replacement?
items.answer: Four key factors drive pricing: engine code (EJ25 vs EJ257), condition (used vs reconditioned vs rebuilt), vehicle complexity (STI or H6 models cost more), and supply‑only vs supply‑and‑fit. Location matters too — London and Southeast labour rates run 20–30% higher. Our comparison tool shows transparent, itemised quotes so you see exactly what you're paying for.
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Subaru engine specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Subaru?
items.answer: In most cases, yes — especially if the chassis, gearbox and AWD system are solid. A rebuilt Subaru engine with upgraded components (e.g., MLS head gaskets on EJ25, uprated crank on EE20) often outlasts the original. If your Forester or Outback holds £3,000–£8,000 value, replacement is almost always more economical than buying another vehicle.
items.cta: Find reconditioned Subaru engines with comprehensive warranty cover

items.question: What's included in a Subaru engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: Supply‑and‑fit covers sourcing your exact engine code, removing the old unit, installing the replacement with new fluids/filters, workmanship warranty, safe disposal of the old engine, and a post‑fit test‑drive. Labour typically adds £600–£1,500 depending on model complexity. *Always confirm whether VAT, delivery and ECU reset are included in your quote.*
items.cta: Compare Subaru engine supply and fit deals

items.question: How long does a Subaru engine replacement take?
items.answer: Common models like Forester or Impreza with EJ25/FB25 engines typically take 3–5 working days for a like‑for‑like swap. Complex builds (STI EJ257, H6 EZ36, e‑BOXER hybrids) may require 5–7 days. The biggest variable is engine availability — our network stocks commonly replaced codes to minimise wait times. *Ask suppliers about current lead times before booking.*
items.cta: Use our free comparison tool to see whether a rebuilt or used Subaru engine makes financial sense

items.question: Do reconditioned Subaru engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes — every reconditioned Subaru engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Extended 24‑month coverage is often available on fully rebuilt or remanufactured units. Warranty covers major internals: crankshaft, pistons, timing components, oil pump and cylinder heads. *Always confirm exact terms directly with your chosen supplier before purchase.*
items.cta: Find Subaru engines with 12‑month unlimited warranty

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Subaru engine?
items.answer: Used engines (£800–£2,500) offer lower upfront cost and suit temporary fixes or imminent resale. Reconditioned units (£2,200–£4,800) include new gaskets, bearings and seals, plus warranty protection and should deliver another 100k+ miles. For failure‑prone engines like EJ25 (head gasket) or EE20 (crankshaft), a reconditioned unit with upgraded components is strongly recommended for long‑term reliability.
items.cta: Compare used vs reconditioned Subaru engine prices

items.question: Where can I find Subaru engine specialists near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with a UK‑wide network of vetted Subaru specialists, including recognised hubs in the Midlands, Northwest and Southeast. Our registration lookup instantly matches you with nearby suppliers who stock your specific engine code and offer fitting. Nationwide delivery is available if no local specialist holds your required unit.
items.cta: Find Subaru engine specialists near me with live stock

items.question: What are the most common Subaru engine failures?
items.answer: The four most catastrophic failures driving replacement are: EJ25 head gasket failure (1999–2011 NA 2.5L Forester/Outback), EE20 boxer diesel crankshaft fracture (2008–2016), EJ257 piston ringland/rod bearing failure (STI turbo), and FA20 valve spring recall/timing tensioner wear (BRZ/WRX). Early detection rarely prevents these from becoming terminal — replacement is usually the most reliable solution.
items.cta: Get quotes for engines addressing these common Subaru failures

items.question: How can I get the best price on a Subaru engine?
items.answer: Compare multiple quotes — prices for the same EJ25 or FB25 code can vary by £1,000+ across suppliers. Consider used for tight budgets, but choose reconditioned with upgraded parts for long‑term ownership. Ask about supply‑only if you have a trusted garage, and always verify whether fitting, delivery and VAT are included. *Our free comparison tool does the legwork so you don't miss hidden savings.*
items.cta: Use our free tool to compare Subaru engine prices now
